summary: BM-033 fit cleanly as a harness/reporting slice: reuse the existing benchmark families and make the planning story explicit in task selection and summarize output instead of inventing new task repos.
ergonomics: `benchmarks/run-benchmark.mjs` already had reusable comparison builders, so adding one more scorecard layer stayed local and avoided schema churn.
`benchmarks/test-series-summary.sh` is a strong regression surface because synthetic result fixtures let new reporting paths be tested without running real harnesses.
Full verification is expensive because benchmark prep recompiles several Clasp workspaces through bootstrap recovery paths; expect long, noisy runs even for harness-only changes.
follow_ups: Introduce dedicated benchmark tasks for semantic-memory freshness and parallel-agent lease coordination so BM-033 no longer depends on `clasp-lead-segment` workflow-assistance runs and `control-plane` as proxies.

If more scorecards are coming, factor the summary printing in `run-benchmark.mjs` into a small declarative formatter layer; the current hand-built print paths are still manageable but getting repetitive.
Consider a smaller benchmark-only verification entrypoint for harness/reporting work so full `verify-all` is reserved for final confirmation rather than every iteration.
warnings: The `agent-planning` alias intentionally mixes mirrored language pairs with Clasp-only tasks; any consumer assuming every alias is purely mirrored will be wrong.

The transactional rollback slice only appears in `agent-planning-scorecard` when scenario results for `clasp-durable-workflow` are included in the summary filter; filtering only `codex` runs will omit that slice by design.
The scorecard output depends on notes-series grouping, so synthetic fixtures or real runs must share the same note prefix for the planning slices to roll up together.
residual_risks: The new scorecard maps BM-033 planning dimensions onto existing benchmark families; if the program later adds dedicated lease-coordination or cheapest-path tasks, this aggregation should be updated to point at those direct tasks instead of the current proxies.

`scripts/verify-all.sh` still emits the existing dirty-tree and Node experimental/module warnings during benchmark prep and scenario runs; they did not fail verification, but they remain noisy surfaces.
